paper_title: Using DeepSpeed and Megatron to Train Megatron-Turing NLG 530B, A Large-Scale Generative Language Model

raw_extraction.innovation: Training of the largest monolithic transformer-based language model to date with 530 billion parameters using a scalable 3D parallelism system combining data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl (2020-50, 2021-04), Books3, OpenWebText2, Stack Exchange, PubMed Abstracts, Wikipedia, Gutenberg (PG-19), BookCorpus2, NIH ExPorter, ArXiv, GitHub, Pile-CC, RealNews, CC-Stories
raw_extraction.application: Natural language generation, zero-shot and few-shot task transfer, in-context learning, language understanding
raw_extraction.research_problem: Scaling up language model size to achieve superior zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot learning performance in natural language processing tasks
raw_extraction.hardware_used: NVIDIA A100 GPUs (80-GB), 560 DGX A100 nodes, NVLink, NVSwitch, HDR InfiniBand network, three-level fat-tree topology
raw_extraction.extension: 3D parallelism combining data, tensor, and pipeline parallelism enables efficient training of massive models on thousands of GPUs with optimized memory and compute usage
